title: PM Lane 162 - Local Pilot Launch Follow-Up Packet Export
objective: Reduce Project Miner Temp Power post-standup relay burden by adding a local-only follow-up packet that packages PM Lane 161 capture-sheet prompts into copy/paste review-return sections for VS Code Codex, Desktop Codex closeout review, and sidecar scout review without persisting notes or admitting any write path.
changes: Added Export Pilot Launch Follow-Up Packet to the Field Prep Outputs rail.

Added a browser-local JSON builder with follow-up packet kind/version, PM Lane 161 capture-sheet lineage, review-return sections, orchestration review slots, review-return rules, inherited no-go checks, next packet options, authority boundary flags, and blocked boundaries.

Extended the focused PM import-intake smoke to prove the new download, PM Lane 161 lineage, blank copy/paste review-return sections, orchestration review slots, inherited no-go checks, blocked boundaries, no follow-up packet localStorage key, and mutationRequests=0.
Used a read-only sidecar to review the intended Lane 162 shape, naming risks, and no-write guardrails while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
validation: rg -n "PM Lane 162|Export Pilot Launch Follow-Up Packet|pilot-launch-follow-up-packet|pm_lane_162_local_pilot_launch_follow_up_packet_v1|mutationRequests\)\.toHaveLength\(0\)" PROJECT_STATUS.md docs/operations ops/agents apps/operations-web
validation_result: PASS - operations-web typecheck passed; operations-web build passed; focused Playwright pm import-intake smoke passed; packet JSON parsed; PM Lane 162 guardrail rg passed; git diff --check passed with only expected CRLF warnings on touched and unrelated markdown files.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar Descartes confirmed the PM Lane 162 review-return shape, recommended copy_paste_review_only sections with null returned values, and warned against owner, due date, action item, commitment, status update, dispatch, submit, persist, sync, create, or authorization wording.
next_recommendation: Keep PM live writes blocked. The next safe PM lane can consume returned Desktop Codex closeouts for technical-authority review, prepare explicit PM Lane 142 admission review context, or continue local-only launch follow-up packaging without creating approval rows, imports, assignments, schedule/status changes, meeting notes, review-return persistence, customer commitments, or hosted writes.
